mobilePay.title: Setting Up WeChat Pay & Alipay
mobilePay.desc: Since 2024, both WeChat Pay and Alipay accept foreign credit/debit cards (Visa, Mastercard, etc.). Here's how to get started:
mobilePay.step1: Download WeChat or Alipay from your app store before arriving in China
mobilePay.step2: Create an account using your phone number (international numbers work)
mobilePay.step3: Go to 'Wallet' → 'Cards' → 'Add a Card' and enter your foreign credit/debit card
mobilePay.step4: Complete identity verification with your passport photo
mobilePay.step5: Start scanning QR codes to pay! Most merchants display payment codes
mobilePay.tip: Alipay's 'Tour Pass' mode is designed specifically for foreign tourists and is often the easiest to set up.

backup.title: Backup Payment Options
backup.cash: Cash (CNY) — Always carry some! ATMs at airports and Bank of China branches accept foreign cards. ¥500-1000 is a good starting amount.
backup.visa: Visa/Mastercard — Accepted at international hotels, large malls, and chain restaurants. Not common at small shops or street vendors.
backup.atm: ATMs — Look for Bank of China, ICBC, or China Construction Bank. They have English interfaces and accept UnionPay/Visa/Mastercard.

street.title: Paying at Markets & Street Food
street.desc: Wet markets, street food stalls, and small shops almost exclusively use WeChat/Alipay QR codes. Some may still accept cash but won't have change for large bills.
street.tip: Screenshot the merchant's QR code if your internet is slow — you can still scan and pay from your gallery. Keep ¥10-20 notes for small vendors who only take cash.

transit.title: 144-Hour Transit Visa-Free Policy
transit.desc: Citizens from 54 countries can enter China visa-free for up to 144 hours (6 days) when transiting through major cities.
transit.ports: Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou, Chengdu, Xi'an, Hangzhou, Nanjing, Qingdao, Xiamen, Wuhan, Kunming, and more. 30+ airports and land/sea ports qualify.
transit.requirements: You must have a confirmed ticket to a third country (not the one you came from). Stay is limited to the designated administrative region.

visaFree.title: Visa-Free Country List (50+ Countries)
visaFree.desc: As of 2025, China offers visa-free entry for citizens of France, Germany, Italy, Spain, Netherlands, Malaysia, Thailand, Singapore, Australia, New Zealand, and many more — for stays up to 15-30 days depending on nationality.
visaFree.tip: Visa policies change frequently. Always verify the latest policy on your country's embassy website or through TotoTrip AI before traveling.

arrival.title: After You Arrive
arrival.step1: Fill out the arrival card on the plane or at immigration (available in English)
arrival.step2: Register at the local police station within 24 hours — if you stay at a hotel, they do this for you automatically
arrival.step3: Keep your passport with you at all times — you'll need it for hotel check-in, train tickets, and SIM card purchase
arrival.warning: The 24-hour registration rule is strictly enforced. If staying at an Airbnb or with friends, you MUST register at the nearest police station yourself.

esim.title: eSIM — The Easiest Option
esim.desc: eSIMs are the most convenient way to stay connected. Buy before you arrive, activate instantly — no physical SIM swap needed.
esim.airalo: Airalo — Popular choice, plans from $5/1GB. Easy setup, works in China with data roaming.
esim.holafly: Holafly — Unlimited data plans from $19/5 days. Great for heavy users.
esim.nomad: Nomad — Affordable China plans with good coverage. 3GB/7 days from $8.
esim.tip: Most eSIM plans use roaming, which means they bypass the Great Firewall — you can access Google, Instagram, and WhatsApp without a VPN!

vpn.title: VPN for Internet Access
vpn.desc: Google, YouTube, Instagram, WhatsApp, and many Western apps are blocked in China. If your eSIM doesn't bypass the firewall, you'll need a VPN.
vpn.rec1: ExpressVPN — Reliable in China, easy to use. Download and set up BEFORE arriving.
vpn.rec2: Astrill VPN — Known for stable connections in China. Offers StealthVPN protocol.
vpn.rec3: Surfshark — Budget-friendly option. NoBorders mode designed for restricted networks.
vpn.warning: Always download and configure your VPN BEFORE entering China. VPN websites and app stores are blocked inside the country.

airport.title: Airport WiFi & Physical SIM Cards
airport.desc: Major airports offer free WiFi (requires phone number verification via SMS). Physical SIM cards can be purchased at China Mobile/Unicom counters in the arrivals hall — bring your passport. Typical tourist plans: 20GB/30 days for ~¥200 ($28).

rail.title: High-Speed Rail (高铁)
rail.desc: China's HSR network is the world's largest and fastest. Trains reach 350km/h and connect all major cities.
rail.step1: Book on Trip.com (English-friendly) or the official 12306 app/website. Trip.com is easier for foreigners.
rail.step2: Use your passport number when booking. Pick up tickets at the station with your passport, or use the e-ticket QR code.
rail.step3: Arrive 30-45 minutes early. Go through security and ID check, then find your platform.
rail.tip: Book popular routes (Beijing↔Shanghai, etc.) 2-3 days in advance. Second class is comfortable and affordable (~$20 for 4.5 hours). First class is ~50% more.

metro.title: City Metro / Subway
metro.desc: Most major Chinese cities have excellent metro systems with English signage. Here's how to ride:
metro.alipay: Use Alipay's Transport QR code — works in most cities without buying a ticket. Open Alipay → Transport → select your city.
metro.card: Buy a rechargeable transit card (交通卡) at any station for ~¥20 deposit. Works on metro, buses, and sometimes taxis.
metro.ticket: Single-journey tokens available at ticket machines (English interface available). Costs ¥3-10 depending on distance.

taxi.title: Ride-Hailing (DiDi / 滴滴)
taxi.desc: DiDi is China's Uber. Download the DiDi app (international version available in English). You can pay with foreign credit cards linked through the app.
taxi.tip: If DiDi isn't working, show the driver your destination in Chinese characters on your phone. Hotel staff can also call taxis for you. Avoid unofficial taxi drivers at airports.

airport.title: Airport to City Center
airport.desc: Every major airport has express metro/train lines, airport buses, and taxi stands. The metro is usually cheapest (¥5-25) and fastest. Airport buses run fixed routes to key areas for ¥20-35. Taxis have regulated meters — expect ¥100-200 to city center.

find.title: Finding Great Restaurants
find.desc: 大众点评 (Dianping) is China's Yelp — the #1 app for finding restaurants with ratings, photos, and menus. Download it and use the camera translate feature for Chinese menus.
find.tip: Sort by 好评优先 (best reviews) and look for restaurants with 4.5+ stars. The photo reviews (图片评价) are incredibly helpful for seeing actual dishes.

order.title: QR Code Ordering (扫码点餐)
order.desc: Most restaurants now use QR code ordering instead of paper menus:
order.step1: Scan the QR code on your table with WeChat or Alipay
order.step2: A digital menu appears — use your phone's translate feature if it's in Chinese
order.step3: Select items, choose quantity and spice level, then submit your order
order.step4: Pay through the same interface. Your food arrives at your table!

allergy.title: Dietary Restrictions & Allergies
allergy.desc: Communication is key for dietary needs. Save these phrases on your phone to show restaurant staff:
allergy.noGluten: 我不能吃麸质 — I can't eat gluten
allergy.noPeanut: 我对花生过敏 — I'm allergic to peanuts

mustTry.title: Must-Try Chinese Dishes
mustTry.item1: 北京烤鸭 (Peking Duck) — Crispy skin, served with pancakes and hoisin sauce. Try it in Beijing!
mustTry.item2: 小笼包 (Xiaolongbao) — Soup dumplings. Best in Shanghai. Careful — they're hot inside!
mustTry.item3: 火锅 (Hotpot) — Interactive dining: cook ingredients in a boiling broth. Chengdu style is spicy!
mustTry.item4: 兰州拉面 (Lanzhou Hand-Pulled Noodles) — Found everywhere. Fresh noodles pulled to order. ~¥15-25.
mustTry.item5: 糖葫芦 (Tanghulu) — Candied fruit on a stick. The perfect street snack!

phrases.title: Essential Chinese Phrases
phrases.desc: These 12 phrases will get you through most daily situations. Save this page for quick reference!

apps.title: Translation Apps
apps.app1: Google Translate — Download Chinese offline pack before arriving. Camera mode translates signs and menus instantly.
apps.app2: Apple Translate — Built into iPhone. Works offline with downloaded languages. Good for conversations.
apps.app3: Pleco — The best Chinese dictionary app. Handwriting recognition, OCR camera, and detailed definitions.
